mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, contradicting the labor-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls signal synchronized consumer sector expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand shocks and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, indicating the interaction lacks incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95.0 on the index scale indicates supply-side overheating, which triggers inflationary wage-price spirals and forces monetary tightening, subsequently compressing equity multiples and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO > 95.0, falsifying the supply-overheat-to-tightening transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently expanding above the historical trend rate directly scales trailing corporate earnings, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10, rejecting the earnings-growth pricing channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI acceleration exceeding 0.2 percent signals persistent inflationary pressure, which compresses real yields and anticipates Federal Reserve rate hikes, thereby reducing discounted equity cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) during periods of accelerating CPI, disproving the inflation-to-valuation compression link.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market normalization, which sustains consumer credit demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns, indicating that labor market improvements are being priced out by concurrent valuation headwinds.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in residential fixed investment exceeding 5 percent, coupled with falling housing starts, signals credit market contraction and reduced consumer wealth effects, which suppress corporate cash flows and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes despite the dual decline in housing and residential investment, falsifying the credit-demand transmission channel.
